events.title: Rodríguez defends Venezuela’s emergency earthquake response as number of bodies expected to soar
events.description: Interim president says unrest will not break out despite anger at official response to the 24 June disaster Venezuela’s interim president has defended her country’s emergency response to the twin earthquakes that have killed more than 3,000 people, vowing the country would not descend into social unrest. Many Venezuelans have expressed anger at what they see as the US-backed government’s inadequate response to the 24 June disaster before international teams arrived. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Rodríguez defends Venezuela’s emergency earthquake response as number of bodies expected to soar

events.title: How the 1986 Mexico World Cup was almost cancelled after a devastating earthquake
events.description: Guardian reports after the disaster told of 5,000 deaths, much of the capital being razed, and doubts about Mexico hosting the finals Mexico last hosted the World Cup in 1986 , but the competition was almost cancelled several months before the start when an earthquake struck the capital, Mexico City, leaving at least 5,000 people dead, 30,000 homeless and much of the city flattened, in one of the worst earthquakes to hit the country. To this day, the death toll remains disputed, with some estimates putting it as high as 40,000 . Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ How the 1986 Mexico World Cup was almost cancelled after a devastating earthquake

events.title: As deadly as war and as powerful as governments, global organized crime remains in the shadows

events.title: Côte d’Ivoire floods kill 59 as west Africa endures torrential rains
events.description: Authorities say rainy season getting deadlier, with Ghana reporting 13 dead and floods hitting Benin, Togo and Nigeria Floods in Côte d’Ivoire have killed 59 people since May, the communication minister told a cabinet meeting in Abidjan. There are fears the toll could further rise as rescue teams continue to search for victims during the rainy season, which runs from May until July, the minister, Amadou Coulibaly, added. events.title: Greece's Thessaloniki threatened by spreading wildfire

The fire ignited a ravine in a mountainous area and spread quickly, with strong winds hampering efforts to contain the fire. According to the country's public broadcaster, a 76-year-old man was arrested on suspicion of starting the wildfire, as he allegedly ignited the blaze after his vehicle produced sparks.

events.title: ‘The situation is terrible’: aid workers on life in Sudanese city pummelled by drone strikes
events.description: El Obeid becomes key battleground in war between Sudan’s armed forces and their paramilitary enemies, the RSF Fatima has lost count of the number of drone attacks on the besieged city of El Obeid in Sudan, but said the attacks this past weekend were the most violent so far. The drones hit schools and fuel stations, killing more than 20 people, including students, she said. “Over the past few months, seeing 40 or 45 drones is the norm. You can literally count them,” said the aid volunteer, whose name has been changed for fear of retribution. Continue reading...

events.title: Overseas education project for women and girls axed by UK after two years
events.description: The programme, aimed at keeping 1m girls in school across Africa, Asia and the Middle East, withdrawn after aid cuts A leading higher education programme, aimed at keeping 1 million girls in school across Africa, Asia and the Middle East, has been axed by the British government just two years after it was announced. The scheme, Strengthening higher education for female empowerment (SHEFE), which was unveiled with some fanfare two years ago by the outgoing Conservative government, had a £45m budget to increase access to higher education for 1 million students worldwide. It has now had its tender withdrawn, the Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO) said. Continue reading...

events.description: One of the few certainties of World Cup football has been rocked after Donald Trump lobbied FIFA to overturn a US red card suspension. The decision has caused outrage but it isn't completely without precedent.
events.ticker_label: 💥 World Cup 2026: Trump, FIFA and the Balogun controversy

events.title: World Cup 2026: England hold on against Mexico while Norway oust Brazil
events.description: The Three Lions defied high altitude and a roaring home crowd to beat Mexico 3-2. Man of the match Jude Bellingham scored twice in the space of two minutes while Harry Kane's penalty kept England ahead of a fierce Mexico team. Meanwhile five-time champions Brazil were knocked-out by Norway, as Erling Haaland did what he does best to seal the win 2-1.
events.ticker_label: 💥 World Cup 2026: England hold on against Mexico while Norway oust Brazil

events.title: El Niño forecast to intensify, increasing likelihood of extreme weather
events.description: More blistering heatwaves and other weather extremes are becoming increasingly likely across the world now and in coming months, linked to strengthening El Niño conditions in the tropical Pacific, the UN World Meteorological Organization (WMO) said on Friday.

events.title: ‘The science is here’: UN chief welcomes first global AI assessment
events.description: Fresh scientific evidence and options for harnessing artificial intelligence (AI) were unveiled Wednesday following the launch of the first global, independent scientific assessment of opportunities, risks and impacts by a pioneering UN expert panel.

events.title: Tens of thousands of far-left protesters clash with police in anti-conservative party riots
events.description: Videos show police deploying batons and anti-riot measures as over 30,000 far-left protesters clashed with officers outside AfD's party conference.

events.title: World leaders, dignitaries pay tribute to America on historic 250th birthday
events.description: King Charles and Pope Leo XIV were among world leaders sending messages to Trump on America's 250th Independence Day. From Jerusalem to the UAE and Japan congratulations were sent.

events.title: Thousands of forgotten WW1 soldiers recognised for first time
events.description: Nearly 10,000 soldiers' names are being added to the official database of war dead, a cherished step for many of their descendants.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Thousands of forgotten WW1 soldiers recognised for first time

events.title: Israel’s Smotrich declares ‘revolution’ in West Bank settlement expansion
events.description: Smotrich declares a settlement 'revolution' as Israel approves 13 new outposts during big increase in settler violence.

events.title: Baby dies after Israeli troops block emergency journey to hospital
events.description: A baby died after his family’s attempt to reach hospital was blocked by Israel at a checkpoint in the occupied West Bank
events.ticker_label: 💥 Baby dies after Israeli troops block emergency journey to hospital

events.title: Retail spending fell in March as consumers pull back
events.description: Spending at US retailers fell in March as consumers pulled back amid recessionary fears fueled by the banking crisis.

events.title: ‘Give him any award, and he’ll come running’: Narendra Modi racks up honours on overseas trips
events.description: Indian prime minister has a habit of collecting awards on his travels, some as their first and only recipient As Narendra Modi touched down in Seychelles over the weekend, the archipelago nation in the Indian Ocean swiftly bestowed one of its “highest” honours upon the Indian prime minister. Modi beamed as he accepted the Guardian of the Blue Horizon award from Patrick Herminie, the Seychelles president, complete with a trophy and certificate. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘Give him any award, and he’ll come running’: Narendra Modi racks up honours on overseas trips

events.title: Weapons of long-ago conflicts still haunt people of Laos
events.description: As decommissioning teams seek out unexploded ordnance in paddy fields, forests and rural pastures, Laos prepares to host a review conference of the Convention on Cluster Munitions.

events.title: Sri Lanka prison clashes leave dozens dead
events.description: More than two dozen people have died and over 100 injured following a violent dispute between different prison populations in Negombo.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Sri Lanka prison clashes leave dozens dead

events.title: Frenchman convicted in missing body case confesses to killing his wife, lawyer says
events.description: Cédric Jubillar, a painter and plasterer who was sentenced last October for killing his wife Delphine, has finally confessed to the murder from his prison cell, his lawyer said Monday. The 38-year-old said he was ready to tell investigators the location of his wife's body, which was never found.

events.title: Ukraine must 'get the support it needs': allies prepare for NATO summit
events.description: "Allies must ensure that Ukraine receives the support it needs in its war with Russia" according to NATO chief Mark Rutte, ahead of a summit set to take place in Ankara, Turkey. He has also said allies will be announcing contracts worth tens of billions to "deter and defend". FRANCE 24's Kethevane Gorjestani tells us what to expect from the summit, and what it means for Ukraine.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine must 'get the support it needs': allies prepare for NATO summit

events.title: France at 'turning point' on how to prevent and deal with sexual violence
events.description: In a new twist in a femicide case that has gripped France for years, Cédric Jubillar has admitted to the murder of his wife and has told authorities he would help them find her body. Delphine Jubillar had disappeared in late December 2020 as she was asking for divorce and had started a new relationship. FRANCE 24 Carys Garland tells us more about the case, and the growing tension in France over the killing of women and children.

events.title: Russian spy plane dropped sonar trackers in path of Royal Navy aircraft carrier
events.description: A Russian spy plane dropped tens of sonar devices in the path of the UK's flagship aircraft carrier in a suspected attempt to harass the warship that prompted RAF fighter jets to scramble.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Russian spy plane dropped sonar trackers in path of Royal Navy aircraft carrier

events.title: China fires a missile from a submarine in a test launch in the South Pacific, state media say
events.description: China fires a missile from a submarine in a test launch in the South Pacific, state media say
events.ticker_label: 🪖 China fires a missile from a submarine in a test launch in the South Pacific, state media say

events.title: Invisible peacebuilders: How ‘diplomacy works’ to prevent conflict
events.description: Described as “sometimes modest, sometimes historic,” UN special political missions have quietly been preventing the escalation of conflicts during the organization’s 80-year history, becoming a key instrument for maintaining peace.

events.title: West Virginia man pleads guilty to making violent threats against Trump, ICE
events.description: Cody Lee Smith admitted to lobbing threats against President Donald Trump and ICE agents, according to the U.S. Attorneys Office for the Northern District of West Virginia.

events.title: NYPD detective, children shot in separate Brooklyn shootings over holiday weekend
events.description: NYPD Detective Robert Karroll was shot in his ballistic vest in Crown Heights as four children were among eight shot in Coney Island over the holiday.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 NYPD detective, children shot in separate Brooklyn shootings over holiday weekend

events.title: Zelenskyy pressures US and Europe for more 'air defense' assistance amid ongoing war with Russia
events.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy urges the U.S. and Europe to provide more missiles to help Ukraine amid the war with Russia.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Zelenskyy pressures US and Europe for more 'air defense' assistance amid ongoing war with Russia

events.title: Six Kurdish fighters killed in IRGC ambush as clashes spread across western Iran
events.description: The PDKI says six Kurdish fighters were killed in an Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corp ambush near Piranshahr, while the Revolutionary Guards claim five Kurdish militants are dead.

events.title: Marine Le Pen appeal verdict: Why this moment matters for France
events.description: The leader of France's National Rally leads the opinion polls ahead of the 2027 presidential election and will now find out if she can stand.

events.title: Is this a breakthrough moment for soccer in the US?
events.description: Victory for the national team against Belgium on Monday could mean the sport takes further strides among American audiences.

events.title: Why an anti-sacrilege law in India's Punjab has sparked controversy
events.description: The dispute has revived one of Punjab's most sensitive political and religious issues ahead of state elections next year.

events.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color
events.description: When customers at Silicon Valley Bank rushed to withdraw billions of dollars last month, venture capitalist Arlan Hamilton stepped in to help some of the founders of color who panicked about losing access to payroll funds.

events.title: China tests long-range missile in South Pacific in move Australia condemns as ‘destabilising to region’
events.description: Launch comes just hours after Australia and Fiji sign defence agreement as expert says timing not a coincidence China has conducted a long-range missile test in the South Pacific just hours after Australia signed a defence agreement with Fiji, sparking condemnation from Canberra and regional leaders. The Australian foreign minister, Penny Wong, said the missile test was “destabilising” to the region, while her New Zealand counterpart, Winston Peters, described it as “deeply concerning”. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 China tests long-range missile in South Pacific in move Australia condemns as ‘destabilising to region’

events.title: Hong Kong's 'sound of silence': 6 years under the national security law
events.description: Six years after Beijing imposed its National Security Law, many of Hong Kong's opposition voices have gone silent. Danny Vincent looks at what's changed.

events.title: Philippine Senate launches impeachment trial of Sara Duterte
events.description: The trial against Sara Duterte is the latest escalation in a bitter feud between the Philippines' leading political dynasties. The proceedings could have major implications for the country's 2028 presidential elections.

events.title: Chinese Tianwen-2 space probe reaches asteroid for sampling
events.description: After a yearlong flight of a billion kilometers, a Chinese space probe is set to take samples from an asteroid. The mission is part of what President Xi Jinping calls the country's "space dream."

events.title: Germany news: Federal Cabinet approves draft 2027 budget
events.description: The budget draft provides for more borrowing than initially planned, as well as a big increase in defense spending. Meanwhile, three men are facing trial over a massive stash of illegal weapons. Follow DW for more.

events.title: In pictures: Marine Le Pen’s political rise and frustrated presidential ambitions
events.description: French far-right figurehead Marine Le Pen could see her 40-year political career come to an end on Tuesday if French courts rule that she is guilty of embezzling public funds and ineligible to stand for the presidency in 2027. FRANCE 24 takes a look at how she rose to prominence in France and brought her National Rally party into mainstream politics.

events.title: ICE makes nationwide arrests of illegal immigrants, murderers, pedophiles in holiday blitz
events.description: ICE arrested illegal immigrants convicted of murder, child sex crimes and drug trafficking over the Independence Day weekend as deportation ramps up.

events.title: Folarin Balogun's ban is lifted by FIFA, allowing U.S. striker to play Belgium
events.description: In an unusual move, FIFA's Disciplinary Committee announced that it would delay Folarin Balogun's one-game red card ban. It came after President Trump spoke to FIFA President Gianni Infantino.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Folarin Balogun's ban is lifted by FIFA, allowing U.S. striker to play Belgium

events.title: Trump confirms call at centre of red card controversy 'with massive political consequences'
events.description: President Donald Trump confirmed he asked FIFA boss Gianni Infantino to review the "horrible" decision to hand a red card to star US striker Folarin Balogun but said he did not request it be overturned. Belgium, who the US will be playing in the quarterfinal, have called the decision 'incomprehensible' and a 'a blatant violation of the most basic rules of football and sport'. FRANCE 24's Selina Sykes tells us more and whether this could change Belgium's chance of winning.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump confirms call at centre of red card controversy 'with massive political consequences'

events.title: Tanzanian authorities arrest dozens ahead of anti-government protests
events.description: Tanzanian authorities have arrested dozens of people ahead of planned anti-government protests

events.title: A safe return home: Tackling stigma on Uganda's Ebola frontline
events.description: As Uganda works to contain an Ebola outbreak that is also affecting neighbouring Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), health workers are doing more than responding to a deadly virus. They are helping families navigate fear, misinformation and stigma.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 A safe return home: Tackling stigma on Uganda's Ebola frontline

events.title: 'Mayhem' erupts in wealthy coastal enclave as hundreds arrested in massive takeover during July Fourth weekend
events.description: Videos show fireworks launched in streets and possible looting at a Pavilions store as over 400 arrests were made during the Balboa Peninsula chaos.

events.title: Delta flight struck by fireworks while landing in Chicago on Fourth of July, FAA investigates
events.description: A fireworks mortar struck a Delta Air Lines Airbus A319 carrying 52 passengers as it descended into Chicago Midway Airport on the Fourth of July.

events.title: Waltz calls out Iranian diplomat at UN following drone strikes on Bahrain and Kuwait
events.description: Mike Waltz held up photos of drone and missile strike damage in Bahrain during a tense U.N. Security Council exchange with Iranian diplomat Iravani.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Waltz calls out Iranian diplomat at UN following drone strikes on Bahrain and Kuwait

events.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
events.description: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

events.title: Florida Republican says deporting Haitians with TPS would be ‘huge mistake’
events.description: ‘Haiti is a failed state’, says Carlos Giménez, congressman and Miami Cuban exile, after controversial court ruling Carlos Giménez, a Republican congressman from Florida, broke with the Trump administration on Sunday, calling on the White House to reconsider its push to eliminate temporary protected status (TPS) for Haitian migrants. Returning about 350,000 Haitians to their chaotic, dangerous homeland following the US supreme court’s ruling that the Trump administration can cut off temporary legal protections, would be a grave error, Giménez said. Continue reading...

events.title: Founder of prominent underground church released from prison in China
events.description: Ezra Jin, founder of Zion Church, lands in the US and reunites with family who are ‘overwhelmed with joy’ The founder of one of China’s most prominent underground churches has been released from prison and reunited with his family in the United States. Ezra Jin, the founder of Zion Church, landed in the US on Friday evening. He was one of dozens of church members who were detained in a sweeping crackdown on Christians in October . Continue reading...

events.title: Accused Charlie Kirk assassin Tyler Robinson fights back against state's evidence and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

events.title: Couple publicly caned after alleged TikTok kiss sparks outrage in Indonesia
events.description: A viral TikTok livestream led to a public caning for a young couple in Indonesia's Aceh province after they were convicted of kissing during a TikTok livestream.

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
events.description: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
events.description: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

events.title: Child protection services contacted before four-year-old’s alleged murder as police investigate cannibalism claim
events.description: Police charge woman with murder after boy’s body was found in a Central Coast home on Saturday Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The New South Wales department in charge of child protection received “a number of reports” about a four-year-old child before he was allegedly murdered by his mother, the minister revealed, as she announced a review into how the case was handled. Kate Washington, the NSW minister for communities and family, announced the independent review into the child’s case on Monday afternoon, two days after police found the preschooler’s body at a home on the Central Coast. Continue reading...

events.title: One Nation to contest WA byelection triggered by Labor MP’s resignation – as it happened
events.description: This blog is now closed China tests long-range missile in South Pacific in move Australia condemns as ‘destabilising to region’ My colleague Josh Butler has a full report into today’s controversy surrounding Anthony Albanese’s podcast appearance. You can read the full debrief here: Meta executives to speak at royal commission on antisemitism today Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 One Nation to contest WA byelection triggered by Labor MP’s resignation – as it happened

events.title: Chinese pastor freed after Donald Trump raised issue of his release with Xi Jinping
events.description: A Chinese pastor has been released from jail after Donald Trump intervened to get him freed, in what his family have described as a "miracle".

events.title: Racism significantly impacts access to sport: Independent expert
events.description: From high participation costs and restrictions on Palestinian athletes – to bans on Muslim women wearing hijabs – racism continues to shape who gets to participate in sport, a new UN report published on Thursday has found.

events.title: Big-box stores could help slash emissions and save millions by putting solar panels on roofs. Why aren't more of them doing it?
events.description: As the US attempts to wean itself off its heavy reliance on fossil fuels and shift to cleaner energy sources, many experts are eyeing a promising solution: your neighborhood big-box stores and shopping malls.

events.title: Archaeologists uncover ancient Byzantine city in Egypt’s western desert
events.description: Well-preserved fourth-century quarters reveal details of daily life, urban development and economic activities Archaeologists in Egypt have uncovered a well-preserved Byzantine-era city in the western desert. The fourth-century quarters had residential and religious structures, including a basilica-style church in the Dakhla oasis. Archaeologists also found coins, pottery fragments and tools. Continue reading...

events.title: Why heat waves heighten the risk of blackouts
events.description: When extreme heat hits, people turn to AC, but what if the grid fails? From France to the US, heat waves are pushing power systems to the limit, raising blackout risks and energy prices when electricity is needed most.

events.title: Boston: The cradle of the American Revolution
events.description: Americans celebrated their independence on July 4th. But did you know that the revolution that led to the creation of the US began in Boston? We take you on a tour of this revolutionary city.

events.title: Avignon turns 80: Wagner Moura, Isabelle Huppert attend France's oldest theatre festival
events.description: FRANCE 24's Olivia Salazar-Winspear brings us all the latest from the Avignon performing arts festival, which is celebrating its 80th anniversary. This year, Golden Globe winners, Brazilian actor Wagner Moura and French actress Isabelle Huppert are starring in shows. The festival has also chosen Korean as its language of honour and turns the spotlight on artists from the Mediterranean region. Salazar-Winspear also talks to the co-director of Avignon's fringe program. It's known as the "off" in French and features more than 1,700 shows performed in public spaces around the city. Finally, she gives us her recommendation for a fringe show that is not to be missed.

events.title: Pilot declares mayday before seaplane comes down in New York City’s East River
events.description: Video shows the Kodiak 100 seaplane floating upright in the East River after a hard landing snapped a wing strut near the NY Skyport terminal.

events.title: Charlie Kirk's family attends hearing for suspect charged with his murder
events.description: Tyler Robinson, 23, is accused of murdering Kirk, a key Trump ally and founder of youth organisation Turning Point USA.

events.description: Albanese has apologised 'unequivocally' after his remarks prompted a backlash.

events.description: The agency says that they "appear to be pressure vessels from a space launch vehicle".

events.description: A 330 million pound NHS contract - and Palantir's wider role in UK government - hangs on Andy Burnham's next move.

events.title: Scientists identify secret ingredient in Leonardo da Vinci paintings
events.description: "Old Masters" such as Leonardo da Vinci, Sandro Botticelli and Rembrandt may have used proteins, especially egg yolk, in their oil paintings, according to a new study.

events.title: 'A definitive backslide.' Inside fashion's worrying runway trend
events.description: Now that the Fall-Winter 2023 catwalks have been disassembled, it's clear one trend was more pervasive than any collective penchant for ruffles, pleated skirts or tailored coats.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
events.description: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

events.title: 'I was up to my waist down a hippo's throat.' He survived, and here's his advice

events.title: Melbourne teacher dies after head clash during suburban football game
events.description: Epping footballer Nathan Fitzgerald, 27, died in hospital after hitting his head during a game in Lalor, club confirms Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A suburban Melbourne footballer has died in hospital after a clash of heads saw him fall to the ground and hit his head on a covered cricket pitch, his club confirmed on Monday night. High school teacher Nathan Fitzgerald, 27, was taken to Royal Melbourne hospital on Saturday after the incident during an Australian rules football game in Lalor in Melbourne’s north, and was said to be receiving “end of life” care. Continue reading...

events.title: Trump won spending promises from NATO last year. This week, he'll try to enforce them
events.description: President Donald Trump is heading to Ankara, Turkey, for the annual NATO summit. Last year, he pushed NATO allies to spend more on defense. This year, his mission is to try to enforce those pledges.

events.title: NATO leaders look for unity as Trump attends annual summit
events.description: As NATO leaders gather for this week's summit in Turkey, Europe is preparing to shoulder more of its own defense. David M. Cattler of the Center for European Policy Analysis explains why.

events.title: Pastor freed from prison in China weeks after Trump requested his release
events.description: A pastor of a prominent underground church who was detained in China in October has been released, less than two months after U.S. President Donald Trump brought up his case when meeting Chinese leader Xi Jinping.

events.title: Senegal's power struggle: Referendum to decide on constitutional reform
events.description: Senegal's constitional reform has caused considerable political uproar. The public will now be asked to vote on the proposed amendments. Under a new constitution, the powers of President Faye would be reduced.

events.title: Spain's 'running of the bulls' festival opens in Pamplona
events.description: Revelers poured red wine and held traditional red scarfs for the "Chupinazo," the opening of a festival in northern Spain where hundreds of people are chased through narrow streets by massive fighting bulls.

events.title: Austrian court convicts 2 former Syrian officials of torture
events.description: A Vienna court has convicted two former Syrian security officials, including the former intelligence chief in Raqqa, for offenses including torture and sexual assault against opponents of Bashar Assad's regime.

events.title: Can India, Europe build a clean energy supply chain beyond China?
events.description: With China dominating every step of the solar energy production chain, governments in India and Europe are looking to reduce their overreliance on a single supplier.

events.title: Hamas to dissolve Gaza governing body
events.description: Officials for the militant organization have confirmed the move, which was agreed to earlier this year in a peace deal with Israel.

events.title: Iran: Millions flock to Ali Khamenei's funeral procession
events.description: Crowds have thronged the streets of Tehran in a procession for Iran's late supreme leader. The huge event is seen as a show of strength by the theocratic regime amid peace negotiations with the US.

events.title: Fashion against fascism: When trademarks deter neo-Nazis
events.description: In Germany, where Nazi symbols are banned, activists are using trademark law to fight far-right merchandise.

events.title: European space telescope Euclid spots oldest quasars yet
events.description: Think of quasars as time machines. Rare but revealing. They are light from the beginnings of the universe that let astronomers investigate how early galaxies formed.

events.title: Tadej Pogacar wins third stage of Tour de France to take yellow jersey from Vingegaard
events.description: Slovenian cyclist Tadej Pogacar won stage three of the Tour de France Monday, taking the yellow jersey from his rival Jonas Vingegaard.

events.title: Does the French right have a chance of defeating the far right in the election?
events.description: Right-wing Edouard Philippe has officially launched his presidential campaign less than a year ahead of the 2027 election in France. Philippe was the first one to say explicitly he would run for France's highest office. Now, polls give the ex-PM the best chance to defeat the far right. FRANCE 24's Marc Perelman tells us more.

events.title: How are the millions attending Khamenei's funeral remembering the late ayatollah?
events.description: The funeral procession for Iran's late supreme leader Ali Khamenei has begun in Tehran. Millions are expected to attend, possibly leading to crowds that could rival those that turned out for his predecessor nearly four decades ago. FRANCE 24's Reza Sayah went to the procession and tells us why so many admired the late ayatollah.

events.title: Dissolution of Hamas's Gaza governing body won't bring 'significant change of life'
events.description: Hamas announced on July 6 the dissolution of the body that has governed the Gaza Strip for nearly two decades. The decision would clear the way for a technocratic committee to implement civilian rule. But questions remain. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky tells us more from Jerusalem.

events.title: As a new heatwave looms, Europe falls for Chinese air conditioners
events.description: Hundreds of people besieged supermarkets in and around Paris, with scuffles and shouting matches breaking out as residents scrambled to get their hands on bargain air-cooling units before the next heatwave. With the hunt for affordability, Europeans are turning to Chinese AC units - despite trade tensions between Beijing and Brussels. FRANCE 24's Yena Lee tells us more.

events.title: Hamas dissolves Gaza governing body, clearing way for technocratic committee
events.description: No description available.

events.title: 'Restraining order needed': Trump escalates Meloni feud
events.description: Donald Trump has escalated his feud with Giorgia Meloni with a new goading social media post.

events.title: Son of woman murdered in Crete 17 years ago says fight 'not over' as killer still free
events.description: The son of a Scottish woman who was murdered in Crete 17 years ago has vowed the fight for justice is "not over" as the killer remains free pending an appeal.

events.title: Car seats: The mystery of our depopulating planet
events.description: 👉Listen to Stuff Matters on your podcast app👈

events.title: Island-wide blackout hits Cuba in latest blow to fuel-starved island
events.description: Island-wide blackout hits Cuba in latest blow to fuel-starved island

events.title: UK sanctions Russian labs and people over chemical weapons used on Navalny, Skripal
events.description: Britain has imposed sanctions on nine Russian people and entities for developing chemical weapons

events.title: Reform UK leader Nigel Farage faces questions over donations from convicted fraudster
events.description: Nigel Farage is facing mounting scrutiny over undeclared financial gifts including a 5 million pound donation from a Thailand-based cryptocurrency billionaire

events.title: A timeline of Marine Le Pen's political career and the rise of the French far right
events.description: French far-right leader Marine Le Pen's political future is on the line as a Paris appeals court rules on her eligibility to stand in the 2027 presidential election

events.title: Sky agrees to buy British broadcaster ITV for up $2.1B to compete with streaming
events.description: Sky, the UK-based TV, internet and mobile phone provider owned by Comcast, has agreed to buy ITV’s media and entertainment arm for up to 1.6 billion pounds, which is $2.1 billion

events.title: Gold rings around 2,000 years old found during dig at Thailand archaeological site
events.description: Officials in Thailand have discovered two gold rings that are around 2,000 years old

events.title: Australia, Fiji seal new mutual defense pact in push to counter China in the Pacific
events.description: Australia and Fiji have signed a new bilateral defense alliance in a second major diplomatic win within a year for Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese against Chinese influence in the South Pacific

events.title: Poor ventilation and children packed in, UK's outdated schoolhouses swelter in heat
events.description: Over 1,000 schools across the U.K. have been forced to close during Europe's recent record-smashing heat wave

events.title: Canada to decide between German, South Korean submarine bids on Monday
events.description: Canada is expected Monday to choose between German and South Korean bids to build a multibillion-dollar fleet of 12 submarines

events.title: Philippine Senate opens the impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte
events.description: The Philippine Senate has started the impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte in a politically volatile event that will unfold against the backdrop of her feud with President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. Thousands of police officers were deployed to s...

events.title: Pope on vacation after decisive flexing of papal muscle
events.description: Pope Leo XIV has had a whirlwind finale to the first half of 2026.

events.title: Man accused of mowing down two girls in alleged double murder exposes twisted reason he kept streaming
events.description: Vincent Battiloro allegedly struck Maria Niotis and Isabella Salas on electric bikes, then laughed about engagement on a YouTube livestream the next day.

events.title: Accused Charlie Kirk assassin Tyler Robinson fights back as prosecutors' sprawling case comes into focus
events.description: DNA on the murder weapon, alleged text confessions, and Discord messages take center stage as Tyler Robinson faces a week-long preliminary hearing.

events.title: Toddler declared dead after near-drowning was found alive in hospital morgue hours later, police say
events.description: An toddler survived after being mistakenly declared dead following a near-drowning, where police say his parents failed to supervise him. Police recommended child abuse charges.

events.title: Manson murders allegedly tied to CIA mind control experiments: congressional testimony
events.description: Newly discovered CIA records are being declassified as House Republicans probe alleged links between MKUltra mind-control experiments and Charles Manson.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Manson murders allegedly tied to CIA mind control experiments: congressional testimony

events.title: Conservative Keiko Fujimori officially declared winner of Peru's presidential runoff election
events.description: Keiko Fujimori won Peru's presidential runoff after defeating Roberto Sánchez by a razor-thin margin, pledging an "iron fist" against surging crime.

events.title: 11-year-old dies from rabies after bat landed on his face while he was sleeping
events.description: An 11-year-old boy in Ontario died of rabies after a bat landed on his face as he slept, according to the Canadian Medical Association Journal.
events.ticker_label: 💥 11-year-old dies from rabies after bat landed on his face while he was sleeping

events.title: Vatican excommunicates breakaway Catholic bishops who defied Pope Leo XIV
events.description: Pope Leo XIV excommunicates Society of St. Pius X bishops after unauthorized consecrations, declaring the traditionalist Catholic movement in schism.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Vatican excommunicates breakaway Catholic bishops who defied Pope Leo XIV

events.title: Khamenei body in cold storage as feared Basij mobilizes ahead of historic Iran funeral
events.description: Tehran prepares massive security operation for Ali Khamenei's July 9 burial, mobilizing Basij militia and IRGC for expected historic turnout.

events.title: Families sue Maduro in US, accuse ex-president of directing police unit tied to extrajudicial killings
events.description: Families of five Venezuelan men filed a lawsuit accusing Nicolás Maduro of overseeing FAES, a police unit allegedly responsible for extrajudicial killings.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Families sue Maduro in US, accuse ex-president of directing police unit tied to extrajudicial killings

events.title: Russian generals' assassinations expose growing rift inside Putin's security apparatus
events.description: Internal frictions between the FSB and Russian military are growing as generals demand protection amid a wave of assassinations, sources say.
